title: FAMILY CAREGIVER SUPPORT PROGRAM - 211info
text: MAIN OFFICE FAMILY CAREGIVER SUPPORT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about available services, assistance in accessing these services, individual counseling, support groups, and caregiver training. Respite care and supplemental services are available on a limited basis. 800 N Bayshore Drive Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: -Unpaid caregivers who meet one of the following: -Adult family members or other informal caregivers providing care to individuals 60 years of age and older -Adult family members or other informal caregivers providing care to individuals of any age with Alzheimer’s disease and related disorders -Older relatives (not parents) age 55 and older, providing care to children under the age of 18 -Older relatives, including parents, age 55 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 269-2013 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 269-2013 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Coos County Services: Adoption and Foster/Kinship Care Support Groups Caregiver Counseling Caregiver Training * Kinship Caregivers Caregiver/Care Receiver Support Groups Family Caregiver Subsidies Respite Care Subsidies Specialized Information and Referral * Kinship Caregivers Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - AREA AGENCY ON AGING FOOD - HOME DELIVERED MEALS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OPTIONS COUNSELING INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE – MEDICAID TRANSPORTATION - ASSISTED TRANSPORTATION TRANSPORTATION - TRANSPORTATION PASSES If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. MAIN OFFICE
